questions.question: 1. Which clustering algorithm is specifically designed to handle pure categorical data (e.g., Colors, Cities, Shapes)?
options: A) K-Means
B) K-Medoids
C) K-Modes
D) CLARANS

questions.question: 2. How does the K-Modes algorithm measure the "distance" between two categorical data points?
options: A) By calculating the Euclidean distance between their letters.
B) By calculating a mismatch score (counting how many categories are different).
C) By finding the theoretical mean of the text strings.
D) By using the Silhouette Score.

questions.question: 3. In the K-Modes algorithm, how is the center of a new cluster updated?
options: A) By calculating the mathematical average of all rows in the cluster.
B) By selecting the data point closest to the origin (0,0).
C) By finding the most frequently occurring value (the mode) in each column for that cluster.
D) By randomly picking a new row from the dataset.

questions.question: 4. What is a known weakness of the K-Modes algorithm?
options: A) It is too slow to use on large datasets.
B) It cannot be used if the dataset has more than 3 columns.
C) It treats all mismatches equally, completely ignoring the natural order of ordinal categories (like "Low", "Medium", "High").
D) It forces the user to calculate Euclidean distances manually.

questions.question: 5. You are analyzing a healthcare dataset that contains a patient's Age and Blood Pressure (numbers), as well as their Blood Type and Smoking Status (categories). Which algorithm is explicitly built for this dataset?
options: A) K-Means
B) K-Modes
C) K-Medoids
D) K-Prototypes

questions.question: 6. How does K-Prototypes calculate the total distance between two rows of mixed data?
options: A) It converts all text to numbers and uses Euclidean distance.
B) It converts all numbers to text and counts the mismatches.
C) It calculates Euclidean distance for the numbers, mismatch scores for the text, and adds them together.
D) It uses K-Means on the first half of the data and K-Modes on the second half.

questions.question: 7. In the K-Prototypes algorithm, what is the purpose of the Gamma ($\gamma$) parameter?
options: A) It dictates the total number of clusters ($k$) the algorithm will find.
B) It is a weighting factor that balances the influence of the categorical mismatches against the numerical distances.
C) It automatically deletes outliers from the dataset before clustering begins.
D) It decides how many random samples CLARA should take.

questions.question: 8. If you set the Gamma ($\gamma$) weight in K-Prototypes extremely high, what will the algorithm focus on the most when grouping data?
options: A) The numerical distances
B) The categorical matches/mismatches
C) The outliers
D) The Silhouette Score

questions.question: 9. During the "Update" step of K-Prototypes, how is the new hybrid center (the prototype) created?
options: A) It finds the Mean for the numerical columns and the Mode for the categorical columns.
B) It finds the Mode for the numerical columns and the Mean for the categorical columns.
C) It selects an existing data point to act as a Medoid.
D) It randomly generates numbers and text until the error is zero.

questions.question: 10. Which of the following is the best real-world use case for K-Prototypes?
options: A) Grouping images of cats and dogs based purely on pixel color values.
B) Grouping students based purely on their "Pass/Fail" status and "Major".
C) Grouping retail shoppers based on their Annual Income ($) and their preferred Membership Tier (Silver/Gold/Platinum).
D) Finding the fastest delivery route between 10 different city coordinates.
